manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: applesauce, breasts, crate, flyswatter, corn, bathrobe, cement, ladder, chopstick.
Your opponent's team words are: dentist, hairnet, vase, parrot, bunny, hair, tent, fig.
Distractor words are: headphone, binoculars, lollypop, parrotfish, tongs, sardine, eyedropper.
The kill words are: cellphone.

message.content:
CLUE: construction
TARGETS: cement, ladder, crate
message.reasoning:
The task: Provide a single-word clue and list of target words (our team's words) that clue relates to. Must be semantically related, not be any of the words in any list or contain parts of them. Must be one word clue. Provide "CLUE: <WORD>" and "TARGETS: <TARGETS>" only.

We need to choose clue that relates to some of our team's words: applesauce, breasts, crate, flyswatter, corn, bathrobe, cement.

We must avoid clue being any of the listed words, also cannot contain parts of them: e.g., "apple" contains part of "applesauce"? Probably not allowed because part of the word. Safer to avoid any substring of any listed word. So we cannot use "apple", "corn", "crate", etc. Also can't use "fly", "swat", etc. So better choose something else.

We need to consider clue that connects multiple target words. Maybe "farm" relates to applesauce, corn, maybe crate (since crates used on farms), maybe cement? Not really. "Breakfast" could relate to applesauce, corn (cornflakes), maybe breast? No.

We can maybe give clue "construction" relates to cement, crate? Ladder? Also cement, ladder, crate maybe. But clue "construction" might also hint at "building" etc.

We can pick "kitchen" relates to applesauce, corn (corn on the cob), perhaps crate (storage), maybe brew? Not sure.

Another idea: "bedroom" relates to bathrobe, maybe breasts? Might be problematic.

We must avoid kill word "cellphone". That's fine.

We must avoid distractor words being guessed accidentally. The clue should not be too ambiguous leading to opponent words: dentist, hairnet, vase, parrot, bunny, hair, tent, fig; distractors: headphone, binoculars, lollypop, parrotfish, tongs, sardine, eyedropper.

Choose clue "DIY" maybe ties to cement, ladder, crate, flyswatter? Not quite.

Maybe "summer" relates to corn, applesauce, maybe crate (picnic), bathrobe (warm). Still ambiguous.

Better: "construction" relates to cement, ladder, crate maybe. Also "flyswatter" not related. But we can pick those three: cement, ladder, crate. That's three words. That's good.

Clue "construction" is not part of any listed words. No substring of any target. So okay.

Will "construction" inadvertently hint at opponent words? dentist, hairnet, etc. Not likely.

Thus answer:

CLUE: construction
TARGETS: cement, ladder, crate

Make sure format exactly.
